other_observers: The bird was seen by many other birders during its stay.; Don't know
description: The bird was a medium-sized Buteo, intermediate in size between Red-tailed and Broad-winged Hawks. It was seen briefly in flight and its’ wings were a bit longish, longer than that of a Broad-winged. When perched, the bird looked less stocky than a Red-tailed and it had a longer-appearing tail. The upperparts were a dark brown overall, except for scattered white feathers across the scapulats and coverts. The primaries were a dark brown and the secondaries were brown interrupted with tan barring. The primary tips extended to near the tip of the tail, but the tail was still longer. The tail was rectangular when perched and displayed 4-5 dark brown bands separated by light tan-whitish bars. The terminal tip of the tail was whitish. The head was rounded and dark brown with a thin, light supercilum and the bill was relatively short and deeply hooked on the upper mandible. The irides were yellow. From the front, the bird was basally whitish-cream with dark, tear-dropped shaped streaking throughout.Behaviors: The bird was noted briefly in flight flying low through the cottonwoods. It then perched along the side of a cottonwood for the remainging length of my observation.Call: None heardPlumage: 1st cycle (Second Year)
